textSpans.text: 1 would like to refer my patient, FRANK MENDOZA for home health evaluation and treatment
Below are my patient's current demographics for your reference:
Please feel free to contact pur office at (702) 982-6402 if you have any questions.
Provider: Chamian, Noel M, MD (Supervisor: Chamian, Noel M, MD;
Patient encounter documented using a qualified (non-CCHIT) certified EHR (In-House)
Patient refused to have pulmonary consult
Smoking Status: Nonsmoker Patient encounter documented using a qualified (non-CCHIT) certified EHR
G8448 Patient encounter documented using a qualified (non-CCHIT) certified EHR (In-House)
G8448 Patient encounter documented using a qualified (non-CCHIT) certified
The diagnosis for this patient is Age related debility.
G8553 At least one Rx created at encounter was generated/sent electronically w/ a qualified eRx system (In-House)
G8752 Most recent systolic BP <140 mmHg (Send-Out)
G8754 Most recent diastolic BP <90 mmHg (Send-Out)
J61 Pneumoconiosis due to asbestos and other mineral fibers
RESPIRATORY: normal respiratory rate and pattern with no distress; decreased breath sounds in the RLL; ; expiratory
or other stimulants/decongestants in common cold remedies, and perform routine monitoring of blood pressure with
He is tolerating the medication well without side effects.
meclizine 25 mg oral tablet, chewable [chew 1 tablet (25 mg) by oral route 3 times per day pRN]
amLODIPine 10 mg oral tablet [take 1 tablet (10 mg) by oral route once daily]
clopidogreL 75 mg oral tablet [take 1 tablet (75 mg) by oral route once daily]
eszopiclone 1 mg oral tablet [take 2 tablets (2 mg) by oral route once daily at bedtime]
atorvastatin 40 mg oral tablet [take 1 tablet (40 mg) by oral route oat night]
aspirin 81 mg oral tablet, delayed release (enteric coated) [take 1 tablet (81 mg) by oral route after breakfast]
ranolazine 500 mg oral Tablet, Extended Release 12 hr [take 1 tablet (500 mg) by oral route 2 times per day]
bisacodyL 5 mg oral tablet, delayed release (enteric coated) [1 tablet once a day prn]
RECOMMENDATIONS given include: go to the ER if symptom/s worsen, explained potential side effects of medication/s,
hairless area behind 1 ear every 72 hours]
EYES: lids and conjunctiva are normal; pupils and irises are normal;
E/N/T: normal external ears and nose;; normal external auditory canals and tympanic membranes;
PSYCHIATRIC: mental status: alert and oriented X 3; appropriate affect and demeanor; recent and remote memory are
BMI documented PHQ-9 Quick depression assessment done.
CARDIOVASCULAR: normal rate and rhythm without murmurs; normal $1 and S2 heart sounds with no S3, S4, rubs, or
GASTROINTESTINAL: no masses palpated; nontender; no organomegaly
LAB ORDERS: CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, lipid panel, TSH, urinalysis, uric acid.
SKIN: no ulcerations, lesions or rashes no skin thickening, induration, or subcutaneous nodules;
Pt screen for fall risk; document no falls in past year or only 1 fall w/o injury in past year (GER) (In-House)
No history of fall only 1 fall without injury within the past year.
